summary: Project DNA is an AI-powered CLI tool that creates a persistent, local-first architectural knowledge layer for software projects, bridging the gap between business intent and technical reality to reduce AI hallucinations and technical debt.

evidence: React-oriented dependency or source convention detected

architectureStyle.reasoning: The system operates as a CLI tool executed inside software projects, storing knowledge locally in a `.pdna` directory and abstracting the AI provider layer.

dependencyIntent.reasoning: Dependencies support CLI operations, schema validation, AI provider interaction, and local file system management.

businessIntent.overview: Project DNA is an AI-powered architectural governance platform that preserves technical and business knowledge of software projects. It acts as a persistent knowledge layer to help AI coding assistants understand project architecture, reducing hallucinations and technical debt.
goals: Preserve architectural DNA across project lifecycle
Reduce AI hallucinations and architectural inconsistencies
Provide reliable architectural knowledge for AI agents
Bridge the gap between business intent and technical reality
businessIntent.coreValue: Creating a continuously evolving architectural memory that enables AI assistants to generate context-aware and architecturally consistent solutions.

patterns: Prefer TypeScript for new implementation work
Keep architecture knowledge centralized in .pdna files
architectureRules: Provider abstraction: AI layer is decoupled from specific LLM providers
Local-first architecture: Knowledge stored inside the project, not external databases

securityConcerns.title: Secret Management
securityConcerns.description: Avoid storing secrets in source files.

securityConcerns.title: Sensitive Project Context
securityConcerns.description: Treat architecture knowledge as sensitive project context.

riskAreas.reason: While abstracted, the system currently relies on Fireworks AI for reasoning.
False positives in framework detection (e.g., React detected for a CLI tool).

missingContext.reason: No database technology detected or mentioned; relies on local file system.
No authentication mechanism mentioned for the CLI tool itself.
Distribution method for the CLI (e.g., npm registry) not explicitly detailed.

recommendedConstraints: Maintain provider abstraction to allow easy integration of new LLM providers
Ensure all AI-generated output is validated against predefined schemas before persistence
Keep all knowledge storage local to the project repository

importantModules.name: CLI Commands

reasoningSummary: Project DNA is a TypeScript CLI tool designed to capture and structure software project architecture. It merges scanner-based technical facts with human-provided business overviews, using an AI reasoning engine to generate a persistent, local-first knowledge base. The architecture emphasizes provider abstraction and schema validation to ensure reliable context for future AI coding assistants.

architecturalRecommendations.title: Implement Provider Interface
architecturalRecommendations.description: Formalize the LLM provider abstraction layer to ensure Fireworks AI, OpenAI, and others can be swapped seamlessly.
architecturalRecommendations.rationale: Overview explicitly states provider abstraction is a core principle.

architecturalRecommendations.title: Schema Versioning
architecturalRecommendations.description: Implement versioning for the `.pdna` knowledge base schemas.
architecturalRecommendations.rationale: Overview mentions future project versioning and compatibility across future versions.

notes: High confidence in business intent and core architecture based on detailed overview
Scanner facts align with CLI tool nature despite React false positive

scannerSignals: Dependencies include commander, zod, openai, fs-extra
overviewSignals: CLI application with pdna init and pdna project overview commands
Provider abstraction with Fireworks AI as current engine
